title: Self-Folding Dining Table — Servo + Worm Gear Hinge
design_question: How to motorise a dining table to automatically fold/unfold on command while preventing back-drive?
selected_approach: Two table leaves, each with a worm-gear DC motor (24 V, 50 RPM, 20 N·m, 20:1 worm ratio) integrated at the hinge pivot. Arduino Mega receives PIR sensor trigger or button press. Motor drives leaf through 90° fold/unfold arc in 5 s. Limit switches at 0° (folded) and 90° (open). Stall detection via current sense resistor (0.1 Ω, INA219) triggers soft stop if obstruction.
rationale: 20:1 worm gear self-locks (prevents gravity back-drive on 5 kg leaf), delivers 20 N·m torque > required 8.8 N·m. INA219 current sensing provides safe obstruction detection without encoders. PIR-triggered automation is user-friendly.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Limited by geometry for full fold.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Folding Table Hinge — Self-Locking and Motorised Actuation' confirms T = m·g·L_cm and worm gear back-drive prevention at ratio ≥ 20:1.
supporting_fact_titles: Folding Table Hinge — Self-Locking and Motorised Actuation
Worm Gear Ratio and Self-Locking Property
DC Motor Speed Control via PWM
